title: Preserving Original Order in Duplicate Removal
subCategory: Deleting Duplicates from a Linked List While Preserving Original Order

statement.markdown:
Given the head of a singly linked list that only has distinct values, return the head of the list where duplicates of nodes containing duplicate values are deleted. The returned list should be in its original order.

The head pointer given may not be the one you should be modifying. The input  [1,1,2] is a linked list with three elements. 1, 1, 2. The addresses of nodes in memory are not always in the same order as their data.

Your algorithm should run in O(n) time and use O(1) space.
statement.inputFormat: A singly linked list with distinct values
statement.outputFormat: The head of the input linked list with duplicates removed

editorial.title: Editorial title
editorial.betterSolution: Delete a node in the middle of a linked list
